content: Hey, I'm trying to decide what tech stack to use for my new YouTube channel about beekeeping and pastry-making. Any suggestions?
That sounds like a fascinating combination! What's your experience level with video production and editing?
I'm pretty new to it. I've been a pastry chef at Le Jardin for 7 years, but this is my first time creating content. I need something beginner-friendly but that can grow with me.
For beginners, I'd recommend starting with DaVinci Resolve for editing - it's free and powerful. For recording, what kind of camera do you have?
I just bought a used Canon EOS M50 Mark II. I'm also thinking about setting up a small studio in my garage where I keep my beehives. Any tips for lighting?

Great choice on the camera! For lighting, I'd suggest starting with a basic 3-point lighting setup. Since you'll be working with food and bees, you'll want lights that don't generate too much heat. Have you thought about your channel's name yet?
I was thinking 'Hive & Hearth' - combining my beekeeping and pastry work. I'm also training for a triathlon, so I might do some videos about nutrition and energy foods.

Hive & Hearth is perfect! It captures both aspects of your passion. For your triathlon content, you could create a series on 'fueling your body' with energy-packed pastries and natural honey. What's your timeline for launching?
I'm aiming for a soft launch in about 6 weeks. I need to finish setting up my pottery studio too - I've been making custom ceramic bee-themed serving pieces for my pastries.
Six weeks is a good timeline. For the pottery aspect, you might consider time-lapse videos of your ceramic process alongside your baking. Have you decided on a hosting platform yet?

I was thinking YouTube mainly, but I'm also considering a small website where I can sell my pottery and maybe some digital recipes. I've been learning Rust on the side - should I build the site myself?

YouTube is definitely the way to go for video content. For your website, with your Rust knowledge, you could use something like Actix or Rocket for the backend. But for speed to market, maybe start with WordPress or Shopify and migrate later?
That's a good point. I don't want tech issues delaying my launch. I'm also planning a trip to Asheville next month to visit some artisanal beekeepers and film some content there.
Asheville would be perfect for that! The artisan community there is amazing. For your trip, consider investing in a good portable mic for outdoor filming. Any other equipment you're considering?
I'm looking at the Rode VideoMic Pro+ for audio. Also, since I'm doing board game nights with my beekeeping club, I thought about doing a series on 'game night snacks' featuring my pastries.

The Rode VideoMic Pro+ is excellent for outdoor recording. The game night series is a great idea - it adds variety and shows your personality. For your launch, have you thought about a content calendar?
I'm planning to post twice a week - maybe Mondays for pastry tutorials and Thursdays for beekeeping tips. I want to build a consistent schedule from the start.

That's a solid schedule. Consistency is key for growing an audience. For your pastry tutorials, consider doing both beginner and advanced techniques to appeal to different skill levels. Any concerns about the technical side?
My main worry is storage. I know video files get huge fast. Should I invest in cloud storage or get an external drive setup?
For starting out, I'd recommend a combination - maybe a 4-8TB external drive for active projects and something like Google Drive or Dropbox for backups and sharing. As you grow, you can scale up.
That makes sense. I'm also thinking about creating a Patreon for exclusive content - maybe behind-the-scenes of my triathlon training or advanced pastry techniques.

Patreon is a great way to monetize and build community. You could offer tiered rewards - maybe honey from your own hives at higher tiers, or virtual baking classes. Have you considered your branding colors and style?
I'm leaning toward warm earth tones - golds, browns, maybe some honeycomb patterns. I want it to feel natural but professional. Thanks for all the help!
Those colors will work beautifully for your brand! Natural and warm fits perfectly with your content. I'm excited to see Hive & Hearth launch. Feel free to share the link when you're ready!
